checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?
Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: Goodwill=125585344.11 (2010-04-03) growing to Goodwill=169600635.63 (2015-03-28), a ~35% increase over 5 years vs revenue growth of ~30% over same window
OtherAssetsNoncurrent=224880332.56 (2010-04-03) vs OtherAssetsNoncurrent=237453240.27 (2015-03-28), only ~5.6% growth over 5 years, well below revenue growth
IntangibleAssetsNetExcludingGoodwill declined from 45012904.74 (2011-07-02) to 13576909.06 (2015-03-28), consistent with amortization not capitalization buildup

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.location: Income Statement / Cash Flow Statement, FY2014 10-K
Income Statement / Cash Flow Statement, FY2011 10-K

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: GrossProfit=1236903048.68 / SalesRevenueNet=1952164045.93 (FY2009) = 63.4% margin, rising to GrossProfit=1776455291.79 / SalesRevenueNet=2531131061.10 (FY2014) = 70.2% margin
Gross margin continued expanding (64.9%->66.0%) even in FY2012 when SalesRevenueNet declined from 2385686080.49 (FY2011) to 2308939067.26 (FY2012)

InventoryNet=139078142.77 (2010-04-03) vs SalesRevenueNet=1952164045.93 (FY2009) = 7.1% ratio, jumping to InventoryNet=281870984.08 (2011-04-02) vs SalesRevenueNet=2522721085.83 (FY2010) = 11.2% ratio

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.location: NetIncomeLoss and Revenues, comparative annual data

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3569900.88 (2015-03-28) against AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=262568179.71 (2015-03-28), a 1.36% ratio, down from 1.60% three years earlier despite AR growing 14.7% over the period

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filing_date 2006-08-10, plus recurring CORRESP/UPLOAD sequences (SEC comment letters) in 2006-2011 and 2013-2014

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Annual data available continuously from FY2009 (period 2009-03-29 to 2010-04-03) through FY2014 (period 2014-03-30 to 2015-03-28), with quarterly granularity in most series
evidence.location: Revenues, NetIncomeLoss, and related series across filings

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Under-reserving for doubtful accounts as receivables balance grows, potentially understating bad debt expense and overstating net receivables/earnings modestly; ratio of allowance to gross receivables declined from ~1.60% (FY2012) to ~1.36% (FY2014-15) even as AR grew, which could reflect either genuinely improving collections or a slow-building reserve shortfall.

Persistent gross margin expansion (63.4% to 70.2% over six fiscal years) even in years of revenue decline could reflect aggressive capitalization of costs into inventory/COGS timing, favorable cost allocation, or genuine efficiency gains; cannot be distinguished from the data alone but merits scrutiny given inventory-to-revenue ratio also stepped up sharply in FY2010 (7.1% to 11.2%) coincident with a Goodwill increase suggesting acquisition-related inventory revaluation.

rationale_evidence.quote: InventoryNet=139078142.77 (2010-04-03) rising to InventoryNet=281870984.08 (2011-04-02) alongside Goodwill increase from 125585344.11 to 142221103.53

top_signals: Allowance for doubtful accounts as a percentage of receivables declined from ~1.60% (FY2012) to ~1.36% (FY2014-15) even as gross receivables grew, a mild reserve-adequacy concern
Gross margin expanded steadily and monotonically from 63.4% (FY2009) to 70.2% (FY2014) irrespective of revenue direction, an unusually persistent trend worth further scrutiny

Historical filing irregularities including an NT 10-K (2009-05-28) followed by a 10-K/A (2009-06-03), and two 10-Q/A amendments (2005, 2007), plus multiple SEC comment-letter exchanges (CORRESP/UPLOAD) though these predate the most recent five fiscal years and no amendments appear post-2009
Operating cash flow consistently and comfortably exceeded net income in every fiscal year (FY2009-FY2014), which is a reassuring earnings-quality signal counterbalancing the above concerns
Receivables and soft/intangible asset growth tracked or lagged revenue growth over the full period, with no material multi-year outpacing detected
